name: Choosing & Cooking with Chiles
recipeIngredient: New Mexico Green Pepper (medium to very hot, depending on region)
New Mexico Red Pepper (riped New Mexico Green)
Serrano pepper (green pods are best when fresh)
Fresh Cayenne Pepper (4 to 7 inches in length)
Chile de Arbol (extremely hot, use sparingly)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash all chiles thoroughly to remove dirt.
2. When handling chiles, wear rubber gloves and avoid rubbing your eyes.
3. Roast chiles using one of the following methods:
a. Oven Method: Preheat oven to 450°F, place chiles on baking sheet, roast for 20-30 minutes, turning frequently until evenly blistered and browned. Let cool, then remove skins by rubbing in closed paper or plastic bag.
b. Open Flame Method: Roast over barbecue grill or gas stove at medium heat, turning every couple of minutes until charred. Remove from flame, soak in ice water to remove skins, then discard.
c. Frying Method: Heat 1 inch of vegetable oil in a saucepan, gently drop chiles into hot oil until golden brown, turn with tongs, and drain on paper towels. Peel skins when cool, remove seeds, devein, and rinse.
4. Store prepared chiles in plastic bags in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 6 months.
5. To dry green chiles: Roast using Oven Method, then hang or lay flat outdoors in warm, dry weather for about 4 days, turning daily. Once fully dried, store in a cool, dry place.
6. To reconstitute dried chiles, soak in warm water for 30 minutes, remove stems and seeds, and use as desired.
